name: FINISH THE GAME — Every missing port, every broken feature, everything
description: Comprehensive list of EVERYTHING still wrong, missing, or unported. Built from old codebase catalog, gap analysis, visual playtesting, and governor testing. No new features — just faithfully finishing what should have been done during the rewrite.

userStories.title: Movement system uses A* pathfinding instead of direct teleportation
userStories.description: The old movementSystem used Yuka steering + A* pathfinding via graphBuilder.ts and pathfinder.ts. The new one does direct line-to-target. The nav graph is now built (ba83797) but movementSystem.ts doesn't use findPath(). Port the old movement logic.
acceptanceCriteria: movementSystem reads nav graph from world.runtime.navGraphs.get('main')
When a move order is issued, compute A* path via findPath() from ai/pathfinder.ts
Store waypoints on the entity and follow them sequentially
Units walk AROUND water/impassable tiles instead of through them
Arrival at each waypoint advances to next waypoint
Final waypoint arrival pops the order
Units with canSwim can path through water tiles (cost 2)
Test: unit paths around a water obstacle instead of walking through it
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Wire existing audio engine (src/audio/) into gameplay — not the stub audioRuntime
userStories.description: 1064 lines of Tone.js audio code exist at src/audio/ (engine.ts, music.ts, musicController.ts, sfx.ts, sfxBridge.ts, voiceBarks.ts). The new engine has a stub audioRuntime.ts that doesn't use any of it. Wire the REAL audio.
acceptanceCriteria: src/audio/engine.ts AudioEngine is initialized on first user interaction
src/audio/sfx.ts SFX definitions used for all game sounds (not zzfx stubs)
src/audio/music.ts procedural music plays during gameplay
src/audio/musicController.ts manages music state (menu/combat/briefing)
src/audio/sfxBridge.ts bridges game events to SFX playback
src/audio/voiceBarks.ts unit voice responses play on selection/command
Delete or replace src/engine/audio/audioRuntime.ts and sounds.ts stubs
Audio actually plays in the browser when you click units and issue commands
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Wire existing rank emblems (src/canvas/rankEmblems.ts) into entity rendering
userStories.description: 306 lines of rank emblem rendering code exists. Draws faction badges + 14 unit-type symbols (chevron, star, cross, diamond, etc.). Not used by LittleJS renderer.
acceptanceCriteria: Import drawRankEmblem from src/canvas/rankEmblems.ts
Call it during entity rendering for units with veterancy rank > 0
Faction outline drawn in correct color (green OEF, red Scale Guard)
Unit type symbol drawn at top-right of entity
Visible in game when units promote
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Wire existing portrait renderer (src/canvas/portraitRenderer.ts) into dialogue/briefing
userStories.description: 816 lines of portrait rendering. Renders hero portraits for briefings and command transmissions. 8 portrait definitions exist at src/entities/portraits/. Not used.
acceptanceCriteria: Briefing overlay shows commander portrait from portraitRenderer
Command transmission panel shows speaker portrait
All 8 portraits render correctly (Bubbles, FOXHOUND, Whiskers, Splash, Fang, Marina, Muskrat)
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Wire existing gesture detector (src/input/gestureDetector.ts) into LittleJS input
userStories.description: 289 lines of multi-touch gesture classification. Tap, long-press (500ms), 1-finger drag (selection), 2-finger drag (camera pan), pinch (zoom). LittleJS handles basic mouse→touch but not RTS-specific gestures.
acceptanceCriteria: GestureDetector classifies touch input into tap/long-press/drag/pan/pinch
Touch tap = select unit (same as mouse click)
Touch long-press + drag = box select
Two-finger drag = camera pan
Pinch = zoom
These work on actual mobile device (test with Chrome DevTools mobile emulation)
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Use building tile PNGs for building rendering instead of colored rectangles
userStories.description: 21 building tile PNGs exist at public/assets/tiles/buildings/ (burrow.png, barracks.png, command_post.png, etc). Buildings render as flat green/red rectangles.
acceptanceCriteria: Load building PNGs during gameInit
Each building type renders with its actual tile image
Building images scale with camera zoom
Construction progress shows as partial opacity of the building image
Building labels still visible below the image
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Use resource/prop tile PNGs instead of colored circles for resources
userStories.description: Resource PNGs (salvage_cache.png, intel_marker.png, supply_crate.png) and prop PNGs (forest tiles, campfires, etc) exist but aren't used. Resources render as colored circles.
acceptanceCriteria: Load resource and prop PNGs during gameInit
Mangrove trees render with forest tile images, not green circles
Fish spots render with appropriate water/fish imagery
Salvage caches render with salvage_cache.png
Props (bushes, rocks) from tilePainter scatter are already rendering via terrain chunks
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Old FSM AI states (src/ai/fsm/) wired into enemy behavior
userStories.description: 433 lines of FSM states exist at src/ai/fsm/states.ts with context.ts (104 lines), profiles.ts (163 lines), runner.ts (96 lines). The new aiSystem has its own FSM but doesn't use the Yuka-backed FSM runner.
acceptanceCriteria: Review src/ai/fsm/states.ts — determine if its logic is already in the new aiSystem or adds new behavior
If the old FSM has states/transitions the new aiSystem lacks, port them
If the new aiSystem already covers everything, document why the old files aren't needed
Enemy units behave correctly: patrol, detect, chase, attack, flee, group assist
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Skirmish AI opponent (src/ai/skirmishAI.ts) wired into skirmish mode
userStories.description: skirmishAI.ts implements a difficulty-based AI opponent that builds workers, barracks, army, and attacks. Not used in new engine.
acceptanceCriteria: Skirmish mode creates an AI opponent using skirmishAI logic
AI builds economy (workers, resource gathering)
AI builds military (barracks, army composition 60% melee / 40% ranged)
AI attacks when army threshold reached
Difficulty setting (Easy/Normal/Hard/Brutal) affects AI timing and resources
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: EventBus game events wired to HUD alerts, audio, and visual feedback
userStories.description: The old game used EventBus for 18+ event types (under-attack, building-complete, training-complete, enemy-spotted, etc). The new engine uses world.events but many events are never generated or consumed.
acceptanceCriteria: Systems emit events to world.events for: unit-death, building-complete, training-complete, research-complete, under-attack, enemy-spotted, resource-depleted, objective-complete, objective-failed
HUD AlertBanner consumes these events and shows alerts with world position
Audio sfxBridge plays appropriate SFX for each event type
Clicking an alert snaps camera to event location
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Old keyboard hotkeys (src/input/keyboardHotkeys.ts) fully wired
userStories.description: Hotkey bindings exist: H=halt, A=attack-move, P=patrol, Esc=cancel, Ctrl+1..9=control groups, 1..9=recall, Space=center on alert. Some are partially wired in tacticalRuntime but not all.
acceptanceCriteria: H key halts selected units (clear order queue)
A key enters attack-move mode (next click issues attack-move order)
P key enters patrol mode
Escape cancels current mode / deselects
Ctrl+1..9 assigns selected units to control group
1..9 recalls control group
Space centers camera on last alert
All hotkeys visible in ActionBar button labels
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Minimap input (src/input/minimapInput.ts) fully functional
userStories.description: minimapInput.ts converts minimap canvas clicks to world coordinates. Partially working but right-click-to-move-units on minimap may not work.
acceptanceCriteria: Left-click on minimap jumps camera to that world position
Right-click on minimap moves selected units to that world position
Drag on minimap pans camera continuously
Minimap shows correct entity positions, fog state, camera rect
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Terrain Y-axis: LittleJS Y-up vs game Y-down coordinate mismatch
userStories.description: LittleJS uses Y-up (positive Y is up) but the terrain grid and entity positions use Y-down (row 0 = top). This may cause terrain chunks to render in wrong position or entities to appear flipped.
acceptanceCriteria: Terrain chunks render at correct screen positions matching entity positions
Entities appear at correct positions relative to terrain features
Camera panning moves in intuitive directions (drag up = scroll up on map)
Minimap orientation matches the main viewport
Test: entity at tile (40,68) appears on the correct terrain tile visually

userStories.title: Building tile images loaded and used for all 21 building types
userStories.description: Building PNGs exist but buildings render as colored rectangles. Load the images and use them.
acceptanceCriteria: All 21 building PNGs loaded: burrow, barracks, command_post, dock, field_hospital, fish_trap, watchtower, armory, gun_tower, minefield, sandbag_wall, stone_wall, flag_post, spawning_pool, fuel_tank, venom_spire, scale_wall, siphon, sludge_pit, great_siphon, shield_generator
Each building entity type maps to correct PNG
Building image rendered at entity position with correct size
Under-construction buildings show at reduced opacity with progress bar
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Governor actually completes Mission 1 — economy, buildings, training, combat
userStories.description: The headless governor currently gathers resources and places some buildings but doesn't complete objectives or win. It needs to train military, attack enemies, and complete all 4 objectives.
acceptanceCriteria: Governor gathers enough timber (150) to complete objective 1
Governor builds Command Post (objective 2)
Governor builds Barracks (objective 3)
Governor trains 3 Mudfoots (objective 4)
Governor achieves victory on Mission 1 within 60000 ticks
PlaytestReport shows victory outcome, resources gathered > 200, buildings built >= 3, units trained >= 3
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: All entity stats come from existing entity definitions — not hardcoded
userStories.description: Entity definitions exist at src/entities/units/, src/entities/buildings/, src/entities/heroes/, src/entities/resources/. These have HP, armor, speed, attack, vision, cost, etc. The spawn pipeline must read from these, not hardcoded values.
acceptanceCriteria: spawnUnit reads HP, armor, speed, attack, vision, popCost from src/entities/units/ura/river-rat.ts etc
spawnBuilding reads HP, armor, buildTime, popCapacity from src/entities/buildings/ura/burrow.ts etc
spawnResource reads yield, resourceType from src/entities/resources/mangrove-tree.ts etc
No hardcoded stat values in missionBootstrap or tacticalSession — all from entity defs
Test: spawned river_rat has stats matching the river-rat.ts definition
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Database initialization doesn't error on page load
userStories.description: initDatabase() is called in main.tsx but still errors sometimes. Ensure it's called before any persistence operation and handles failure gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: No 'Database not initialized' errors in browser console
Campaign progress loads correctly on app start
Settings persist between sessions
Save/load works from pause menu
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: CRT-styled phosphor-green minimap matching old Minimap.tsx
userStories.description: Old minimap had CRT styling: dark bg, green border glow, scanlines, grid overlay, radar sweep animation. New minimap is basic.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimap has dark background with slight green tint
Green border glow effect
Scanline overlay
Entity pips are bright colored dots (green player, red enemy, yellow resource)
Camera viewport rect in white
Click to jump camera works
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: All 16 missions pass governor playtest with correct mechanics
userStories.description: Governor should play all 16 missions without crashing and make meaningful progress. Mission-specific mechanics (convoy, stealth, waves, boss) should activate.
acceptanceCriteria: All 16 missions boot without errors
Standard missions: governor gathers, builds, trains, fights
Commando missions (4, 8, 9, 12): governor uses military units only
Wave defense missions (6, 11): waves spawn correctly
Boss missions (15, 16): boss entity spawns with phase config
No mission softlocks or infinite loops
pnpm test passes (allMissions.test.ts)

userStories.title: Zero console.log in production, zero TODO comments, zero fallback rendering
userStories.description: Final cleanup pass.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -r 'console.log' src/ --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' returns 0 results (only console.error for actual errors)
grep -r 'TODO\|FIXME\|HACK' src/ --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' returns 0 results
No shape/color fallback rendering — if sprite/tile not loaded, entity is invisible until it loads
No debug text on canvas
pnpm build passes with zero warnings
